search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG184 GORD reflux follow-up symptoms alarm endoscopy
site:nice.org.uk CG184 reflux review medicine referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic GORD diagnosis or response classification
automatic PPI, dose, lifestyle advice, endoscopy, surgery, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Current diagnosis and response require clinician review.
No medicine or action is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with established or suspected reflux follow-up; children, pregnancy, dysphagia, bleeding, severe pain, postoperative, and malignancy contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, colorectal, result-review, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE gastroenterology, colorectal, hepatology, laboratory, endoscopy, referral, prescribing, screening, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult GORD section supports interval heartburn, regurgitation, epigastric, meal-related, nocturnal, trigger, medicine, and impact context without confirming ongoing reflux disease.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact alarm section supports clinician-assessed concern, examination, and existing endoscopy context without ordering a procedure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician review of available H. pylori, laboratory, and endoscopy results without interpretation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and annual review for people needing long-term dyspepsia management; medicine names and doses are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician-entered discussion, medicine-review status, safety net, follow-up, and referral documentation without generating treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Diagnostic basis, symptom values, response, adherence, medicine, alarm features, endoscopy, diagnosis, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
